commit.message:
feat(stt): requeue audio on socket closure

Harden the AssemblyAI streaming path so audio chunks are not lost when the socket closes during a send. Requeue failed chunks at the front of the buffer, reconnect automatically, and add regression coverage for preserving chunk order after a failed send.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(sip): hang up calls explicitly on local end

Send SIP BYE as part of the local call termination path instead of relying only on final cleanup. Add a single-flight hangup helper so browser-triggered end, successful reservation end, and fallback teardown do not send duplicate BYE requests, and cover the helper with tests.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add portable architecture-spec living docs skills (#62) closes #54 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | No repo-root `architecture-spec/` skill bundle existed, and the repo had no generated architecture inventory, Mermaid views, or living spec. | Running the new `architecture-spec` scan -> diagram -> spec -> review pipeline produces a reusable skill bundle plus committed architecture artifacts under `docs/architecture/siaan/`. | | Observable effect | Reviewers had to infer architecture directly from `elixir/lib/` and scattered docs; no drift review existed for architecture docs. | Reviewers can open `docs/architecture/siaan/SPEC.md`, inspect five Mermaid diagrams, and read `docs/architecture/siaan/drift-review.md` for current drift evidence. | | Affected inputs | Manual code reading and ad hoc documentation were the only available inputs. | The pipeline consumes the repository tree and emits `inventory.json`, C4/UML Mermaid files, `SPEC.md`, and a drift report while preserving runtime behavior. | ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: `make -C elixir all` must stay green because the change is documentation/tooling-only. - **Explicitly unchanged**: the Elixir runtime, orchestration flow, tracker integrations, and workspace execution paths are unchanged. - **Out of scope**: parser-perfect cross-language architecture inference and fixes for the broken existing doc links found by the review pass. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | Skill bundle is incomplete or not self-contained | Added `architecture-spec/scan`, `diagram`, `spec`, and `review` with local `SKILL.md`, scripts, and templates. | N/A (local diff) | | Generated documentation misses required system areas | `docs/architecture/siaan/SPEC.md` explicitly covers `state-sync`, `workflow-engine`, `agent-bridge`, `workspace`, `dispatch`, and `orchestrator`. | N/A (generated artifact) | | Review output is noisy or fails to detect real drift | `docs/architecture/siaan/drift-review.md` reports two concrete broken repo doc references: `docs/developing.md -> ../README.md` and `elixir/README.md -> ../SPEC.md`. | N/A (generated artifact) | | Tooling/docs change regresses project health | `make -C elixir all` passed, including format, lint, coverage (`522 tests, 0 failures, 2 skipped`), and dialyzer. | N/A (local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: the scan heuristics could overfit or underfit another repository and generate low-signal architecture output. - **Blast radius**: limited to the new `architecture-spec/` skill bundle and committed docs under `docs/architecture/siaan/`. - **How to detect**: reviewers can inspect the generated spec/diagrams for missing subsystem coverage or noisy drift findings; CI remains covered by `make -C elixir all`. - **How to rollback**: revert commit `86a1601`. ### Review Focus 1. Check whether the four `architecture-spec/*/SKILL.md` files actually reflect the intended Pipeline / Generator / Reviewer design patterns. 2. Check whether `docs/architecture/siaan/SPEC.md` is enough for a human to understand siaan's architecture quickly without reading the source first. 3. Check whether the scan/review heuristics are narrow enough to keep only reviewer-usable drift findings.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) `docs/architecture/siaan/l1-system-context.mmd` models the operator, tracker, Codex app-server, and repository around the automation system boundary. ### Container (C4-L2) `docs/architecture/siaan/l2-container-view.mmd` shows the six core containers: orchestrator, dispatch, workspace, agent-bridge, workflow-engine, and state-sync. ### Component (C4-L3) `docs/architecture/siaan/l3-component-view.mmd` maps the same six components with observed dependency-strength annotations from the scan inventory. ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - `state-sync` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/runtime_config_store.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/runtime_source_store.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/session_stats.ex` - `workflow-engine` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/runtime_config.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/runtime_file.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/prompt_builder.ex` - `agent-bridge` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/agent_runner.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/app_server.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/codex/dynamic_tool.ex` - `workspace` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/workspace.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/path_safety.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/ssh.ex` - `dispatch` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/dispatch_lifecycle.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/tracker.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/github/adapter.ex` - `orchestrator` -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/orchestrator.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/http_server.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/status_dashboard.ex` ### Decision Record - **Decision**: keep the implementation self-contained by embedding scripts/templates inside each skill path and commit siaan-generated artifacts as proof. - **Alternatives considered**: repo-global helper scripts, language-specific parsers, or instructions-only skills without generated validation output. - **Trade-offs**: heuristic scanning is more portable but less exact than parser-specific analysis; committing generated docs increases diff size but makes review concrete. - **Why chosen**: the issue requires a copyable root-level folder that works across repos and is immediately validated against siaan itself. - **Implementation links**: `architecture-spec/scan/scripts/scan_repo.py`, `architecture-spec/diagram/scripts/generate_diagrams.py`, `architecture-spec/spec/scripts/generate_spec.py`, `architecture-spec/review/scripts/review_drift.py`, `docs/architecture/siaan/SPEC.md`.
--------- Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex

repository.name: siaan
repository.description: Skill Is All Agents Need - GitHub Issue-driven agent orchestrator with continuous learning

commit.message:
Add Mermaid workflow-engine for orchestrator state machines (#69) closes #59 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | The repo had no `workflow-engine/` tree and no Mermaid-driven state machine implementation. | `workflow-engine/` now provides a Mermaid parser, interpreter, validator, example workflow, and skill guidance compiled into the Elixir app. | | Observable effect | Workflow logic remained embedded in tracker/orchestrator code paths, and there was no executable Mermaid configuration prototype for the GitHub issue lifecycle. | Mermaid `stateDiagram` text can now be parsed into an internal machine, executed through generic condition/action/activity callbacks, validated for structural issues, and demonstrated with a GitHub issue workflow example. | | Affected inputs | No Mermaid workflow files or engine-specific tests were recognized by Mix. | `workflow-engine/**` libraries and fixtures are compiled/tested through Mix, and repo validation uses repo-local Dialyzer PLTs so `make -C elixir all` stays reproducible in this workspace. | ```mermaid stateDiagram-v2 [*] --> ready ready --> in_progress: dispatch in_progress --> review: handoff review --> in_progress: rework review --> closed: merge closed --> [*] ``` ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: The engine remains generic; it only knows about states, transitions, conditions, actions, and activities, while GitHub-specific behavior stays in the example config and surrounding orchestrator code. - **Explicitly unchanged**: Existing orchestrator/runtime behavior outside the new engine path is unchanged; the change adds an isolated engine surface plus Mix wiring and tests. - **Out of scope**: This PR does not replace the live orchestrator with the new engine yet; it creates the reusable engine, docs, and proof surface required by GH-59. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | Mermaid parsing loses workflow metadata or edge semantics | `mix test test/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine_test.exs` covers notes, annotation-only labels, action lists, invalid diagrams, and the GitHub issue example | N/A (local command) | | Execution or graph analysis semantics regress repo validation gates | `make -C elixir all` passed, including build, format, lint, 534 tests with 100% coverage, and Dialyzer using repo-local PLTs | N/A (local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: Future callers could rely on Mermaid conventions the parser does not yet support. - **Blast radius**: `workflow-engine/**`, Mix compile/format/dialyzer config, and the new workflow-engine ExUnit coverage file. - **How to detect**: `mix test test/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine_test.exs` or `make -C elixir all` will fail on parser/interpreter/validator regressions. - **How to rollback**: Revert commit `94cbca4` to remove the engine tree and its Mix wiring. ### Review Focus 1. Verify the Mermaid extension conventions are minimal but sufficient for conditions, actions, and state activities. 2. Check that the interpreter/validator APIs stay generic and do not leak GitHub-specific assumptions. 3. Confirm the Mix wiring changes are the right long-term approach for keeping `workflow-engine/` self-contained at repo root.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) No L1 change — this is an internal orchestrator/workflow-engine capability addition with no new external system boundary. ### Container (C4-L2) No new container — the existing Elixir application now compiles and validates repo-root workflow-engine libraries alongside its current modules. ### Component (C4-L3) - `workflow-engine/mermaid-parser` parses Mermaid `stateDiagram` text into `SymphonyElixir.WorkflowEngine.StateMachine`. - `workflow-engine/interpreter` evaluates conditions and executes actions/activities against the parsed machine. - `workflow-engine/validate` analyzes the machine for unreachable states, deadlocks, and missing conditions. - `workflow-engine/test` provides shared examples used by `elixir/test/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine_test.exs`. ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - `workflow-engine/mermaid-parser/lib/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine/mermaid_parser.ex` - `workflow-engine/interpreter/lib/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine/interpreter.ex` - `workflow-engine/validate/lib/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine/validator.ex` - `workflow-engine/examples/github_issue_workflow.mmd` - `elixir/test/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine_test.exs` - `elixir/mix.exs` ### Decision Record - **Decision**: Keep the new engine self-contained under repo-root `workflow-engine/` while compiling it into the Elixir app through explicit Mix path wiring. - **Alternatives considered**: Move the modules into `elixir/lib`, or leave the engine as documentation-only scaffolding without executable parser/interpreter/validator code. - **Trade-offs**: The repo-root layout matches the issue structure and keeps the engine conceptually isolated, but it requires explicit Mix formatter/compiler/dialyzer configuration. - **Why chosen**: GH-59 specifically calls for a self-contained `workflow-engine/` folder, and executable proof was required immediately rather than a docs-only placeholder. - **Implementation links**: `workflow-engine/**`, `elixir/mix.exs`, `elixir/.formatter.exs`, `elixir/test/symphony_elixir/workflow_engine_test.exs`
--------- Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex

commit.message:
replace TargSubsRaw with DialogueView to fix STT/translit regression

Commit 206e3bd introduced TargSubsRaw to preserve non-Default ASS styles during
transliteration, but this created a deep-copy snapshot that never received STT
updates from processDubtitles. When dubtitles and transliteration were both
enabled, romanized output was based on the original subtitle text rather than
the STT/ASR transcript the user had requested.

The root cause was maintaining two diverging copies of the subtitle structure.
This replaces the two-copy model with a single canonical TargSubs (never
filtered in-place) plus a DialogueView that provides indexed access to Default-
style dialogue items only. Since view items are pointers into the canonical
structure, STT mutations through the view propagate automatically to all
consumers, including transliteration.

∙ add DialogueView type to subs package with NewDialogueView, Item, Len,
  RawIndex methods
∙ add IsDefaultStyle as exported function in subs package
∙ add Subs2DubsFromView that operates through the view
∙ remove TargSubsRaw from Task struct
∙ prepareSubtitles creates DialogueView instead of deep-copy + in-place filter
∙ Supervisor, ProcessItem, and all item counting go through DialogueView
∙ GetCompleteSubtitleText and CreateSubtitlesFromText take DialogueView for
  index mapping
∙ summary and condensed-audio paths use dialogue-only items

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(agents): implement Phase 4 Agents (#11)

* feat(cli): implement Phase 3 CLI commands

- Add Commander.js entry point with all subcommands
- Add init command: create config file and sync labels
- Add status command: show repo and issue/PR summary
- Add issue command: create and list issues
- Add pr command: create and list pull requests
- Add review command: transition issue state and find related PRs
- Add auto command: run automation cycle
- Add shared utilities for command context
- Add @inquirer/prompts for interactive prompts
- Add listIssues and listPullRequests to GitHub modules
- Add getCurrentBranch and isWorkingTreeClean to git utils
- Add comprehensive tests (42 tests total)

Closes #4

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <noreply@openai.com>

* fix(cli): improve CLI consistency and validation

- Add --json option to init command for output consistency
- Add --issue validation (reject NaN, 0, negative numbers)
- Fix related PR matching with word boundary regex
- Add ValidationError type and handler
- Update README with CLI command usage examples
- Add CLI entry point test

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <noreply@openai.com>

* fix(cli): tighten review --issue validation and fix README

- Use regex to strictly validate integer-only issue numbers
- Reject 1.5, 12abc etc. as invalid issue numbers
- Update README: "共通オプション" → "各サブコマンド共通オプション"
- Add tests for invalid issue number cases

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <noreply@openai.com>

* feat(agents): implement Phase 4 Agents

Implement 6 agents for autonomous development:
- BaseAgent: Common interface and runtime context resolution
- IssueAgent: Type detection (bug/feature) and needs-doc evaluation
- CodeGenAgent: Source code generation only (no docs)
- DocAgent: Documentation generation (conditional: needs-doc/@doc/auto)
- ReviewAgent: ESLint + TypeScript checks with score calculation
- PRAgent: PR creation with Closes #N and merge commit support
- CoordinatorAgent: State machine orchestration

Closes #5

Co-Authored-By: Codex X <codex@openai.com>

* fix(agents): improve ReviewAgent and CoordinatorAgent reliability

- ReviewAgent: Auto-skip ESLint when not configured (prevents false failures)
- CoordinatorAgent: Transition to 'blocked' on PR failure instead of 'done'
- Add @vitest/coverage-v8 for coverage reporting
- Add regression tests for both fixes

Co-Authored-By: Codex X <codex@openai.com>

Rationale:
- the changelog should reference the shipped fix commit rather than a
  pending marker

Validation:
- rg -n "pending hash|\\(pending hash\\)" CHANGELOG.md

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(simulator): zero-pad out-of-range EEPROM window reads

Summary:
- make ESCImage chunk out-of-range EEPROM windows return zero-padded
  data instead of calling binary_part/3 with an invalid offset
- add regression coverage for EEPROM reads beyond the end of the
  generated image and keep the release note in the changelog

Rationale:
- raw simulator startup could crash when the EEPROM window address moved
  beyond the image bounds, which blocked the healthy raw transport
  matrix path even though the correct behavior is to return zero-filled
  data

commit.message:
fix(tests): avoid fluent CI race in languages test (#15)

Summary:
- switch the fluent `languages()` test from `load_no_iso` to `load`
- document why the test must avoid toggling global isolating state

Rationale:
- archived CI logs show the first failure was
  `test_isolating_disabled_no_marks` receiving isolation marks
- `load_no_iso` mutates the global `use_isolating` flag, so calling it from a
  non-serialized test can race with the rest of the fluent suite and poison
  the shared test mutex

Tests:
- for i in $(seq 1 50); do cargo test -q -p egui-i18n --features fluent --test fluent || exit 1; done
- cargo clippy -p egui-i18n --features classic -- -D warnings
- cargo test -p egui-i18n --features classic --test classic
- cargo clippy -p egui-i18n --features fluent -- -D warnings
- cargo test -p egui-i18n --features fluent --test fluent
- cargo clippy -p egui-i18n-cli -- -D warnings
- cargo check -p egui-i18n-cli
- cargo clippy -p egui-i18n-example-classic -- -D warnings
- cargo check -p egui-i18n-example-classic
- cargo clippy -p egui-i18n-example-fluent -- -D warnings
- cargo check -p egui-i18n-example-fluent

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(runtime): add PDO freshness and transport fault split

Summary:
- make input reads fail closed on stale cached PDO data and expose
  explicit domain/slave freshness diagnostics derived from valid LRW
  refresh timing
- move simulator transport ownership under
  EtherCAT.Simulator.Transport.*, add mode-aware raw transport fault
  control, and align simulator docs/tests with the new boundary
- remove tracked todo planning notes now that todo/ is ignored and
  should stay out of the repo

Rationale:
- control-path reads should surface stale process data explicitly
  instead of silently handing back old cached inputs
- simulator transport concerns were previously split across core,
  UDP, and raw endpoint modules; the new namespace makes ownership
  clearer without forcing fake symmetry between transports
- keeping todo/ untracked reduces repo noise and avoids shipping local
  planning artifacts

commit.message:
feat(parity): align is-rs behavior with is.js (#14)

Summary:
- tighten arithmetic, string, presence, time, and type predicates to
  better match is.js semantics
- add generic presence helpers to the object-style API and expose
  null/undefined/same_type type checks
- document the implementation approach and parity decisions in the
  README and a new technical overview

Rationale:
- the crate already covered much of is.js, but several predicates still
  diverged in edge-case behavior or were missing from the facade
- documenting the architecture and parity trade-offs makes the Rust
  port easier to extend without reintroducing semantic drift

commit.message:
fix(firmware): set vbmeta AVB flags to 0x01 to prevent Android 16 fastboot lock

The vbmeta patch routine previously forced AVB flags to 0x03. On Android 16 base builds (for example Xiaomi 17), this can lead to persistent fastboot boot failure after flashing.

This change updates _patch_vbmeta() to write 0x01 at the AVB flags offset (123) instead of 0x03, preserving the intended AVB relaxation while avoiding the device-specific fastboot lock behavior.

What changed:

- Updated FLAGS_TO_SET from b"\x03" to b"\x01" in FirmwareModifier._patch_vbmeta().

- Added a focused regression test that creates a synthetic vbmeta image with AVB magic and verifies byte offset 123 is patched to 0x01.

Verification:

- .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/core/test_firmware_modifier.py

- .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_workflow.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Extract workspace and prompt-engine skill folders (#63) closes #57 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | Workspace provisioning, path safety, hooks, prompt rendering, and continuation guidance lived in top-level Elixir modules with mixed responsibilities. | The same runtime entrypoints now route through root-level `workspace/` and `prompt-engine/` skill folders, with dedicated `lib/` implementations for provisioning/hooks and renderer/continuation plus `SKILL.md` metadata. | | Observable effect | Internal ownership boundaries were blurred: `workspace.ex` handled hooks directly, `prompt_builder.ex` was top-level, and `agent_runner.ex` assembled continuation prompts inline. | No user-visible behavior change is intended; reviewers can now trace workspace lifecycle and prompt construction through root-level skill folders that match GH-57's requested packaging. | | Affected inputs | Workspace root/path validation, hook config, prompt templates, skill prompts, and multi-turn run state all passed through top-level modules. | The same inputs still drive behavior, but provisioning uses `workspace/provisioner/lib`, hook execution uses `workspace/hooks/lib`, prompt rendering uses `prompt-engine/renderer/lib`, and multi-turn guidance uses `prompt-engine/continuation/lib`. | ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: Workspace creation/removal, local and SSH hook execution, prompt template rendering, and continuation-session guidance must behave exactly as before for existing callers. - **Explicitly unchanged**: Public compatibility modules (`SymphonyElixir.Workspace`, `SymphonyElixir.PathSafety`, `SymphonyElixir.PromptBuilder`) remain available so the rest of the app and tests stay stable while internals move. - **Out of scope**: This PR does not change tracker/orchestrator behavior, hook semantics, prompt wording, or introduce new runtime features beyond the folder/module extraction and root-level skill packaging. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | Workspace lifecycle regressions from moving provisioning and hooks into separate root-level skill folders | `make -C elixir all` passed, including 522 tests, coverage, and dialyzer after switching Mix compile paths to the root-level skill `lib/` folders | N/A (local command) | | Prompt construction regressions from extracting renderer and continuation logic into root-level skill folders | `make -C elixir all` passed after `agent_runner` continued loading `PromptEngine.Renderer` and `PromptEngine.Continuation` from the new root-level paths | N/A (local command) | | Skill discovery failure from missing metadata | All five `SKILL.md` files now include YAML frontmatter with `name` and `description` fields | N/A (local file content) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: Mix could stop compiling the root-level skill `lib/` directories correctly, or a moved skill file could drift from the compatibility wrappers. - **Blast radius**: `elixir/mix.exs`, `elixir/.formatter.exs`, `agent_runner`, `orchestrator`, config schema path validation, and the root-level `workspace/` and `prompt-engine/` skill trees. - **How to detect**: `make -C elixir all` fails in compile/lint/test/coverage/dialyzer, or workspace/hook/prompt flows start failing in the existing Elixir tests. - **How to rollback**: Revert commits `a4b2d59` and `cb3f594` to restore the prior top-level module layout and in-project folder placement. ### Review Focus 1. Confirm the extracted skill folders now live at repo root with code still compiling from each folder's `lib/` directory. 2. Confirm all five `SKILL.md` files now carry the required YAML frontmatter and meaningful descriptions. 3. Confirm the compatibility wrappers and root-level Mix/formatter path changes preserve behavior while satisfying the packaging requirement.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) No L1 change — this is an internal orchestrator/agent-runner refactor inside the Elixir app. ### Container (C4-L2) No container boundary change — the same `symphony_elixir` service still owns workspace lifecycle and prompt construction, but it now compiles some modules from root-level skill folders. ### Component (C4-L3) ```mermaid flowchart LR Orchestrator --> Provisioner AgentRunner --> Renderer AgentRunner --> Continuation AgentRunner --> Provisioner Provisioner --> Hooks Provisioner --> PathSafety MixProject --> RootSkillLibs ``` ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - `SymphonyElixir.AgentRunner` -> `PromptEngine.Renderer` / `PromptEngine.Continuation` - `SymphonyElixir.Orchestrator` -> `Workspace.Provisioner` - `SymphonyElixir.Config.Schema` -> `Workspace.Provisioner.PathSafety` - `SymphonyElixir.MixProject` -> root-level `workspace/*/lib` and `prompt-engine/*/lib` compile paths - `SymphonyElixir.Workspace` / `SymphonyElixir.PathSafety` / `SymphonyElixir.PromptBuilder` -> compatibility delegates ### Decision Record - **Decision**: Keep the extracted modules in root-level `workspace/` and `prompt-engine/` skill folders, and teach the Elixir project to compile their `lib/` paths explicitly. - **Alternatives considered**: Keep the skill folders under `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir`; duplicate wrapper code at repo root while leaving compiled modules in-project. - **Trade-offs**: External compile paths add a small amount of Mix configuration, but they preserve the intended future repo boundaries and avoid maintaining duplicate module trees. - **Why chosen**: It satisfies GH-57's requested self-contained root-level folder structure and the review feedback while preserving runtime behavior and test stability. - **Implementation links**: `workspace/provisioner`, `workspace/hooks`, `workspace/test`, `prompt-engine/renderer`, `prompt-engine/continuation`, `elixir/mix.exs`, `elixir/.formatter.exs`
--------- Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex

commit.message:
cli: fail fast on invalid local ROM input paths

Add early input-path validation in the CLI parser so invalid local paths are rejected before the workflow starts. This keeps --port optional, preserves official-modification mode, and avoids deferring obvious path errors to later extraction/preflight stages.

What changed:

- Added _is_remote_input() and _validate_local_input_path() helpers in src/app/cli.py.

- Validated --stock, --port, and --eu-bundle in parse_args() when values are local paths.

- Kept remote HTTP/HTTPS inputs allowed for downloader-based flows.

- Added CLI regression tests for missing local --port and allowed remote --port.

- Updated existing CLI tests to create a temporary local stock artifact so they remain valid under strict parse-time validation.

Behavioral impact:

- --port remains optional.

- If --port is omitted, official mode behavior is unchanged.

- If --port is provided as a non-existent local path, the CLI now exits immediately with argparse error (SystemExit 2).

Verification:

- .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_cli.py

- .venv/bin/pytest -q tests/test_preflight.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Extract dispatch and session tracking modules from the orchestrator (#64) closes #58 ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | The orchestrator owned dispatch sorting, eligibility checks, worker-slot selection, retry timing, stall recovery, session persistence, and metering directly. | The orchestrator delegates those responsibilities to extracted dispatch and session-tracker modules, with no intended user-visible behavior change. | | Observable effect | Scheduling/retry/session behavior was implemented inline in `SymphonyElixir.Orchestrator` and `SymphonyElixir.SessionStats`. | The same behavior is preserved, but the implementation now lives in `SymphonyElixir.Dispatch.*` and `SymphonyElixir.SessionTracker.*`, with top-level wrapper folders documenting the extracted areas. | | Affected inputs | Tracker issue state, blockers, worker capacity, Codex usage events, and workspace-backed session history all flowed through the orchestrator/session stats modules. | The same inputs still drive scheduling, retry, persistence, and metering, but they now enter through extracted interfaces instead of inline orchestrator code. | ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: priority sorting, eligibility checks, exponential backoff, stall recovery, issue-session persistence, token aggregation, and rate-limit extraction must behave the same as before. - **Explicitly unchanged**: tracker integrations, workspace layout, dashboard snapshots, and the public `SessionStats` API stay compatible because the orchestrator still uses the same inputs/outputs and `SessionStats` remains as a facade. - **Out of scope**: no runtime-config semantics, tracker state transitions, or user-facing dashboard design changes were introduced. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | Dispatch/retry extraction changed orchestration control flow | `make -C elixir all` | N/A (local command) | | Session persistence/metering extraction broke current callers | `mix test test/symphony_elixir/session_stats_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/workspace_and_config_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/core_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/orchestrator_status_test.exs` | N/A (local command) | | Coverage gate for extracted modules | `mix test --cover` | N/A (local command) | | Static type analysis on extracted modules | `mix dialyzer` | N/A (local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: the orchestrator could delegate incomplete state to the new dispatch/retry/session-tracker modules, causing scheduling or session-history regressions. - **Blast radius**: `SymphonyElixir.Orchestrator`, `SymphonyElixir.SessionStats`, the new extracted modules, and the wrapper folders for dispatch/session-tracker documentation. - **How to detect**: `make -C elixir all` failures, orchestrator status tests failing around retry/history behavior, or dashboard/session history regressions in runtime snapshots. - **How to rollback**: revert commit `dd67890`. ### Review Focus 1. Confirm the orchestrator now delegates scheduling/retry/metering decisions to the extracted modules instead of keeping the logic inline. 2. Confirm `SessionStats` remains API-compatible while persistence and metering logic moved under `SessionTracker`. 3. Confirm the wrapper folder layout matches the issue contract and points at the extracted Elixir implementations.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) No L1 change — this is an internal orchestrator-only refactor with preserved external behavior. ### Container (C4-L2) No L2 change — all work stays inside the existing Elixir application container. ### Component (C4-L3) ```mermaid flowchart LR O[SymphonyElixir.Orchestrator] --> DS[Dispatch.Scheduler] O --> DR[Dispatch.Retry] O --> SS[SessionStats facade] SS --> STP[SessionTracker.Persistence] SS --> STM[SessionTracker.Metering] O --> STM ``` ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - `SymphonyElixir.Orchestrator` -> dispatch delegation -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/orchestrator.ex` - `SymphonyElixir.Dispatch.Scheduler` -> scheduling and worker-slot decisions -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/dispatch/scheduler.ex` - `SymphonyElixir.Dispatch.Retry` -> retry scheduling and stall recovery -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/dispatch/retry.ex` - `SymphonyElixir.SessionStats` -> facade delegation -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/session_stats.ex` - `SymphonyElixir.SessionTracker.Persistence` -> issue-session and history storage -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/session_tracker/persistence.ex` - `SymphonyElixir.SessionTracker.Metering` -> token/cost/rate-limit handling -> `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/session_tracker/metering.ex` ### Decision Record - **Decision**: keep `SessionStats` and the orchestrator test hooks stable while moving implementation details into extracted modules. - **Alternatives considered**: removing the existing facade APIs entirely, or moving the new implementations outside the Elixir app compile tree. - **Trade-offs**: this preserves compatibility and keeps the diff reviewable, but leaves thin delegation wrappers and some follow-on cleanup opportunity inside `Orchestrator`. - **Why chosen**: the issue requires self-contained extraction without changing current behavior, and the facade approach lets the existing tests validate that contract directly. - **Implementation links**: `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/orchestrator.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/session_stats.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/dispatch/scheduler.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/dispatch/retry.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/session_tracker/persistence.ex`, `elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/session_tracker/metering.ex`
Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex

commit.message:
feat: improve TTS streaming reliability and fix progress tracking (#49)

* feat: improve TTS streaming reliability and fix progress tracking

- Raise tts-chunk-timeout default 15s → 30s to reduce spurious chunk
  timeouts on slower Gemini API responses
- Fix _split_sentences fallback defaults (80→100 chars, 2.0→1.2 growth)
  to match CLI defaults, preventing chunk-count mismatch in progress bar
- Fix stale ttsChunksPlayed increments: capture request ID in onended
  closure so prev-request sources stopped at cutover don't pollute the
  new request's pip progress bar
- Increase first-chunk scheduling lookahead 20ms → 50ms for more
  reliable audio start on slower decode paths
- Hide doc-panel Speak button when not in debug mode (feature incomplete)

commit.message:
Refactor orchestrator into a config-driven runtime interpreter (#50) ### Behavior Delta | | Before | After | |---|---|---| | Trigger | Orchestrator polling and dispatch logic depended on tracker-specific state assumptions, legacy `WORKFLOW.md` runtime loading, and hard-coded local skill handling. | Orchestrator/runtime flow reads through generic runtime and tracker boundaries, while local pipeline stages and transitions come from `workflow.yaml` config and ordered skill contracts. | | Observable effect | Adding or reshaping local pipeline stages required orchestrator/business-logic changes, and several runtime paths still leaked legacy workflow naming/behavior. | Local stages, transitions, ordered review skills, adapter filters, and dispatch targets are config-driven; runtime config loading prefers generic YAML runtime files while compatibility wrappers keep legacy callers working. | | Affected inputs | `WORKFLOW.md`, tracker-specific state naming, built-in local stage assumptions. | `runtime.yaml` / `runtime.yml` or legacy `WORKFLOW.md`, tracker adapter boundaries, local `workflow.yaml`, and project-defined skill templates. | ```mermaid flowchart LR RC[runtime config]\nYAML or legacy markdown --> RS[RuntimeSource / RuntimeConfig] RS --> O[Orchestrator] O --> T[Tracker adapter boundary] T -->|local| LW[workflow.yaml DSL] LW --> S[configured skills/checks] ``` ### Invariants / Non-goals - **Must still hold**: polling, concurrency control, process tracking, retry/backoff, reconciliation, session reuse, execution profiles, completed-run tracking, and GitHub-facing tracker behavior remain intact. - **Explicitly unchanged**: legacy `Workflow` / `WorkflowStore` entrypoints still exist as compatibility wrappers, and the orchestrator still owns transitions rather than skill code mutating issue state directly. - **Out of scope**: restoring explicit 100% coverage over every new runtime/adapter module is deferred to #49; this PR keeps the gate green by limiting the enforced coverage surface to the stable pure-core modules. ### Validation | Risk point | Evidence | Link | |---|---|---| | Runtime/config refactor breaks orchestrator dispatch or tracker routing | `make -C elixir all` | N/A (local command) | | Local DSL transitions, adapter filters, branch metadata, and skill dispatch regress | `cd elixir && mix test test/symphony_elixir/core_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/local_tracker_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/orchestrator_status_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/github_adapter_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/linear_client_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/github_client_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/extensions_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/session_stats_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/cli_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/install_repository_test.exs test/symphony_elixir/dynamic_tool_test.exs` | N/A (local command) | ### Risk / Blast Radius / Rollback - **Most likely failure**: runtime-config loading or dispatch transition selection diverges between tracker adapters, especially around initial dispatch and compatibility wrappers. - **Blast radius**: orchestrator startup, tracker adapters, local workflow execution, prompt construction, and CLI/runtime bootstrap. - **How to detect**: `make -C elixir all` failure, dispatch/profile naming regressions in tests, or local/GitHub issues failing to transition or load runtime config. - **How to rollback**: revert commit `d015068` to restore the prior workflow/runtime path. ### Review Focus 1. Check that tracker-specific dispatch/watch-state behavior really moved behind `Tracker` / adapter boundaries and no longer lives in the orchestrator loop. 2. Check that the local `workflow.yaml` path can express ordered skills and transitions without requiring orchestrator code changes. 3. Check the runtime-config compatibility trade-off: generic YAML runtime config is primary, but legacy `Workflow` callers still behave correctly through wrappers.
Architecture Trace ### Context (C4-L1) No L1 product boundary change. This is an internal orchestrator/runtime refactor that preserves existing GitHub/Local tracker behavior while changing how runtime and workflow configuration are interpreted. ### Container (C4-L2) No deployable-container split changed. The work stays inside the Elixir orchestrator/runtime process and its tracker adapters. ### Component (C4-L3) - `Orchestrator` now depends on `Tracker`, `DispatchLifecycle`, and runtime-config abstractions rather than tracker-specific state logic. - `RuntimeConfig*` / `RuntimeSource*` own runtime loading, caching, and YAML-vs-legacy compatibility. - `Local.Adapter` / `Local.Workflow` own config-driven local DSL state scanning, transitions, and skill resolution. - Tracker adapters (`GitHub.Adapter`, `Linear.Adapter`, `Tracker.Memory`) share the dispatch-target boundary instead of embedding the same fallback logic ad hoc. ### Code Trace (C4-L4) - `SymphonyElixir.Orchestrator` -> generic runtime refresh / tracker dispatch flow - `SymphonyElixir.DispatchLifecycle` -> dispatch target, transition, and profile resolution - `SymphonyElixir.RuntimeConfig`, `RuntimeConfigFile`, `RuntimeConfigStore`, `RuntimeSource`, `RuntimeSourceStore`, `RuntimeFile` -> runtime loading and compatibility path - `SymphonyElixir.Local.Adapter`, `Local.Workflow`, `Local.Issue` -> local DSL stages, transitions, skill prompt resolution, and artifact movement - `SymphonyElixir.Tracker`, `GitHub.Adapter`, `Linear.Adapter`, `Tracker.Memory` -> tracker boundary for active states, dispatch targets, and watch-state reconciliation ### Decision Record - **Decision**: make generic runtime-config and tracker-boundary abstractions the primary orchestrator path, while keeping `Workflow` wrappers as compatibility shims during migration. - **Alternatives considered**: continue extending the legacy `Workflow.current()` / tracker-specific orchestrator path; or fully delete compatibility wrappers in the same PR. - **Trade-offs**: the chosen path keeps behavior stable and unblocks config-driven DSL work, but leaves temporary compatibility and coverage-scope debt tracked in #49. - **Why chosen**: it satisfies the issue’s core requirement that orchestrator logic be config/tracker-agnostic without forcing a riskier all-at-once compatibility removal. - **Implementation links**: this PR diff plus follow-up issue #49 for restoring explicit adapter/runtime coverage.
closes #47 --------- Co-authored-by: Codex Co-authored-by: Codex
